summary.intent: Fix multiple rounds of PR review comments on a Rust web service, resolving security and correctness issues in webhook auth, IP allowlisting, and data handling.
summary.outcome: All PR threads resolved across three rounds; 59 tests passing (31 unit + 28 integration) with fixes for X-Forwarded-For forgery, PagerDuty multi-signature verification, CIDR enforcement, foreign_keys pragma, and source_profile immutability.

repo: Server uses `into_make_service_with_connect_info::<SocketAddr>()` so `ConnectInfo<SocketAddr>` (not `Option<ConnectInfo>`) is available as an extractor in handlers.
The `behind_proxy` flag on the gateway config gates whether forwarded headers (X-Forwarded-For) are trusted — mirrors the existing WebSocket origin check pattern.
SQLite foreign key enforcement must be set via `.foreign_keys(true)` on `SqliteConnectOptions` rather than per-query PRAGMA to apply to all pooled connections.
CIDR allowlist enforcement is done before auth check using the `ipnet` crate for proper subnet matching, returning 403 on mismatch.
`source_profile` is read-only after webhook creation — the UI disables the field on edit and the patch struct excludes it.

code.finding: X-Forwarded-For header trust gated on `gw.behind_proxy`; ConnectInfo<SocketAddr> used directly (not Option) for direct deployments.
PagerDuty verifier splits comma-separated `v1=` signature entries and checks each, enabling key rotation support.
Integration tests added for CIDR enforcement and PagerDuty multi-signature (single sig, multi-sig rotation, bad sig).
sourceProfile excluded from patch params on edit; field disabled in UI for existing webhooks.
`ipnet` added as dev-dependency for integration tests requiring CIDR matching.

workflow: Each PR review round introduced new P1 comments triggered by the previous fix — iterative review cycles require anticipating cascading feedback.
Using `Option<ConnectInfo>` as an extractor fails when the router is configured with `into_make_service_with_connect_info`; must use the concrete type directly.
Compilation errors should be fully retrieved before editing to avoid partial fixes that still fail.

friction: ConnectInfo extractor used as `Option<ConnectInfo>` initially, causing a compilation error that required a second edit pass.
Each round of fixes introduced new P1 review comments, requiring multiple commit-push-resolve cycles.
Had to read the same file (auth.rs, server.rs) multiple times due to incremental edits and state uncertainty.
open_items: No explicitly deferred items noted; all opened threads were resolved and tests pass.
